product_updates.title: AI is being used to resurrect the voices of dead pilots
product_updates.content: People used AI on a spectrogram image of cockpit recordings to reconstruct them, forcing the NTSB to temporarily block access to its docket system.

product_updates.title: Google goes for the glitter with disco-ball icons: ‘Are y’all sure you still want this?’
product_updates.content: You can now disco ball-ify your entire Pixel home screen, says Google.

product_updates.title: How VCs and founders use inflated ‘ARR’ to crown AI startups
product_updates.content: Some AI startups are stretching traditional revenue metrics when talking about progress publicly. And their investors are fully aware.

product_updates.title: Elon Musk can’t hear you over the sound of his $1.75 trillion IPO
product_updates.content: The SpaceX S-1 is finally here, and the story it tells goes way further than rockets. The filing runs to 36 pages of risk factors alone, and the numbers inside match the ambition: a $28 trillion total addressable market, a pay package tied to establishing a Mars colony, and a valuation target tha…

product_updates.title: You can no longer Google the word ‘disregard’
product_updates.content: After Google Search's AI update, the word "disregard" now effectively breaks the search interface.

product_updates.title: We tried Google’s AI glasses and they’re almost there
product_updates.content: Google demoed prototype Android XR glasses that overlay Gemini-powered translation, navigation, and other information directly into your field of view.

product_updates.title: SpaceX files to go public, and the math requires a little faith
product_updates.content: The SpaceX S-1 is finally here, and the story it tells goes way further than rockets. technical_breakthroughs.title: Texas AG sues Meta over claims that WhatsApp doesn't provide end-to-end encryption
technical_breakthroughs.content: Critics note a lack of factual support in lawsuit filed by US Senate candidate.

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Download: coding’s future, the ‘Steroid Olympics,’ and AI-driven science
technical_breakthroughs.content: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. technical_breakthroughs.title: A hacker group is poisoning open source code at an unprecedented scale
technical_breakthroughs.content: GitHub is just the latest victim of TeamPCP, a gang that has carried out a spree of software supply chain attacks.

technical_breakthroughs.title: Google I/O showed how the path for AI-driven science is shifting
technical_breakthroughs.content: During Tuesday’s Google I/O keynote, Demis Hassabis, the CEO of Google DeepMind, proclaimed that we are currently “standing in the foothills of the singularity.” It was a striking statement—the singularity is the theoretical future moment when AI rapidly exceeds human intelligence and dramaticall…

technical_breakthroughs.title: The Enhanced Games fit right in with the rest of 2026’s longevity vibes
technical_breakthroughs.content: This Sunday, a group of 42 athletes will gather in Las Vegas to compete in a somewhat unusual sporting competition. Participants in the inaugural Enhanced Games are being encouraged to take performance-enhancing drugs. The goal is to “push the boundaries of human performance.” The games’ organize…
